forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea will maintain a ridge across South Australia through to Friday. Northerly winds freshen on Friday ahead of a front that is forecast to move across the far west Friday afternoon, then continue across the state during the weekend. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to cross south SA and bring a following trough across southern coasts late Sunday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Mostly sunny with cloud developing in the west later. Maximum temperatures well above average. Northeast to northerly winds, fresh in the west during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy, clear in the north and east. North to northeasterly winds.

Showers and possible thunderstorms developing west of about Ceduna. Maximum temperatures well above average. Northerly winds freshening with southwesterly change developing in the west during the afternoon and evening.

A band of patchy rain with possible gusty thunderstorms about the west will move eastwards to reach eastern border districts during the afternoon. Isolated showers persisting about western and southern coasts. Maximum temperatures well above average with fresh and gusty northerly winds ahead of a west to southwesterly change in the west moving east to reach eastern border districts in the afternoon and evening, bringing below average temperatures.

text.p: Showers about the agricultural areas with possible thunderstorms about coasts and the far east. Maximum temperatures generally below average, grading to near average about the eastern border. Fresh west to southwesterly winds extending throughout, becoming gusty at times near the coasts.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Sunday: generally less than 2 mm across the pastoral districts, increasing to 5 mm in parts of the North East Pastoral, 2 to 10 mm about the West Coast district and northern agricultural areas. Scattered areas of 5 to 15 mm across the southern agricultural areas with isolated high falls of 10 to 25 mm possible over parts of the Mount Lofty Ranges and western coastal fringes.
